summary: Render ONE Remotion composition to a transparent qtrle .mov (headless Chrome PNG sequence, then pack).

parity.note: Headless Chrome rasterisation is not byte-reproducible across GPU/driver/font-hinting, so bit_exact would be a lie. What IS guaranteed: frame COUNT, canvas size, fps and alpha channel are exact (asserted in tests/test_op_mograph_render.py), because those are the properties the downstream overlay filtergraph depends on — a drifted frame count desynchronises every later beat, a drifted subpixel does not. This is the honest mode for anything that goes through a rasteriser.

comp.description: Composition id inside the bundle (catalog comp such as `Statement`, or a `Bespoke*` id whose .tsx arrives as the `entry` input). The LLM that AUTHORED that .tsx, and the compile/repair loop around it, are BRAIN and stay on the control plane — this op only rasterises what it is handed.

props.description: inputProps for the composition. Free-form BY DESIGN: these are already brand/plan DATA computed upstream (brandTokens, brandFonts, copy), not encoder tuning. This is the one open sub-object in the registry and it is open because the alternative is mirroring the entire Remotion prop surface into a schema that would rot on every comp change.

duration_frames.description: Omit to use the composition's own exported duration.

concurrency.description: Chrome tabs. A resource knob, not a decision — the runner may clamp it to what the box actually has.
